title: Quarterly Newsletter
description:
Rather than sending a perfunctory weekly email to go unopened like all other boring newsletters, publish a quarterly zine with beautiful production value and in-depth features.

Open with a "Letter from the Editor" by the CEO. Feature a MIDAO client in each issue with a fawning interview like it's a glossy magazine. Showcase MIDAO's latest public service to the islands with a photo spread.

Finish with a hyperlinked roundup of all MIDAO content since the last issue. (Optional: offer print editions to current MIDAO clients.)

title: DAO It Yourself
description:
Illuminate the benefits of DLLCs with a recurring segment wherein we take an existing type of business and look at how it could be better deployed as a DLLC.

This concept could be implemented in any number of formats. I recommend a YouTube-based vlogcast in the style of a call-in radio show. Our host fields pre-recorded "calls" from real viewers with questions about how their business or organization might be suited to a DLLC.

For bonus points, film in a very Marshall Islands setting to drive home the brand identity (and the offshore appeal).

title: Taste Test
description:
Instead of a dry, dime-a-dozen podcast format, have guests on to discuss their work (and ours) over a cooking challenge. Each episode, a Marshall Islander will submit a traditional recipe, and our onscreen team will attempt to recreate it.

For best results, film in NYC (lots of industry guests at hand) and have someone from the Marshall Islands Permanent Mission judge the final result.

Performing an activity engages the audience's mirror neurons in a way that podcasts do not. This is also a nice way to involve the local community back home.

title: Island Questionnaire
description:
In the style of the Proust Questionnaire, pose a list of island-themed questions to a different figure in crypto every month. Fluffy stuff like "What three records would you want to be stranded with on an island?" or "Trunks or speedo?"

Format it as a beautiful one pager, post and tag. More engaging than the usual interview fare, as everyone naturally thinks about what their own answers would be and occasionally replies with commentary.

title: GG: Jekab Tournament
description:
Easy but entertaining side event around any crypto conference. Host a catered jekab tournament with an enticing crypto prize.

Can be produced at any scale, from simple to lavish. Go big by renting out a beautiful indoor space with a full spread, or keep it breezy by staging it in a public park with snack coolers.

title: XOXO
description:
Scavenger hunt that can be held at any time and/or scale. Hide a Marshall Islands postcard with a message from MIDAO in a public area. The first person to find and post it to socials tagging MIDAO wins a lump crypto prize.

Drop a single postcard in a crypto hub every month, or lay out a 10-card scavenger hunt around a big conference. Super flexible activation!

Tagline: XOXO marks the spot

title: Atoll Adventure
description:
Engage both viewers and the local Marshall Islands community by staging a competition show wherein one XOXO postcard has been hidden on each inhabited atoll, and local teams compete to collect the most.

Alternatively, a show version of XOXO can be produced basically anywhere.

title: Beach Lounger Competition
description:
In the style of the Tokyo Space-Out Competition, challenge participants to out-chill each other on a beach, for a prize. Host this low budget event around any seaside conference. Have MIDAO branded beach towels as merch.

For additional impact, film segments of the competition to recut for socials with observational "sports commentators" dubbed over it. (Keep it anon-friendly by offering NO-FILM marked sunglasses to players who don't wish to make an appearance.) Emphasize how using MIDAO leaves customers worry-free.

title: Legal-ease: Dispatches from the Marshall Islands
description: A substack geared toward regulatory professionals. Open with a cheerful note from the compliance officer, feature a brief client interview about how hassle-free their operations are courtesy of choosing a Marshall Islands DLLC, and pepper pleasant snapshots of the islands throughout. Prime opportunity for SEO magic, too.

title: Island Time
description: Recurring livestream simulcast to all relevant social channels. The topic is flexible—format holds the key for impact. Each livestream will take a listenership snapshot and award a small airdrop (USDM1?) to one listener chosen at random. At the end of the year, the listener snapshots are used to award a grand prize (Marshall Islands getaway?) to a random repeat listener.

title: Anonference
description:
Intriguing satellite event to stage around the big crypto conferences like Devcon. Host a fun, phone-free mixer highlighting MIDAO's unique status as the most anon-friendly DAO structure. Provide branded faraday bags as merch—with mandatory use for entry. Even the doxxed will relish an event where everyone is living in the moment.

In the long run, it could even be possible to host a standalone Anonference in various cities or even the Marshall Islands ICC itself.

title: Public Service
description:
Over time, MIDAO should contribute to public service projects in the Marshall Islands. Anything and everything from helping fund beach cleanups and afterschool activities, to establishing local pro bono coding courses or a free electric shuttle service.

We can then highlight these efforts in the quarterly newsletter or short form videos for social channels.
